channel.title: Posts on Welcome to Data2al
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Welcome to Data2al

item.description: Cert List (12 -> 17) CRM Analytics (Completed - Apr) Admin (2022) Platform App Builder (2022) Sales Cloud (2022) Tableau Desktop Specialist (2022) Platform Dev 1 (2022) Service Cloud (2022) Advanced Admin (2023) Platfrom Dev 2 (2023) Experience Cloud (2023) Javascript 1 (2023) (Developer) Data Arch (2023) Sharing and Visibility (2023) **Application Architect (2023) Integration (2024) Development Life Cycle (2024) Identity and Access (2024) **System Architect (2024) ****Technical Architect (2025)
Not a Blog How to Write Articles 04/11/2022 Good Police and Yakuza Principles 04/10/2022 ‘Elden Ring’ - Battle on the Altus Plateau 04/??/2022 A Night with ‘Spencer’ 03/??/2022

‘Elden Ring’ - Battle on the Altus Plateau 04/??/2022 “As I woke at the Site of Lost Grace, I looked across the half-abandoned battlefield in the barren valley below me. I could see Knights and Soldiers strolling through the area. Their stiff movements betray their state of fatigue. Like a madman, I charged into the nearest group of soldiers, hoping to eliminate any early threats before crossing the field. Halberd in one hand, Sacred Seal in the other, I circled the group and rounded up my prey, strafing and poking, trying to thin out the herd.

How to Write Articles 04/10/2022 Failure is the foundation of success. Success is the lurking-place of failure. When you’re writing articles, write them in reverse pyramid. That way when the editor is editing, he can cut from the bottom up when you’re running against a deadline. Some other writing tips include: Always start with the conclusion; Describe the most important things you have to say; Follow up with the supporting details; Sum up by telling readers what you wrote again.

A Night with ‘Spencer’ 03/??/2022 “Diana sits at the dinner table, trying to contain her anger. She steals a raging glance at Charles, who stares back blankly with a sense of mild curiousness in his expression. He was waiting, waiting to see if she dared express her displeasure at a table where the Queen was also present. Diana slowly turned her gaze away. Looking across the table, she sees the ghost of Anne Boleyn, a figment of her imagination.

Police and Yakuza Principles 4/11/2022 I watched a couple episodes of “Tokyo Vice” and found the show to be very intriguing. The book author had some very good life advices that he learned from both the Yakuza and the Police. I have listed those advices below and added comments explaining each one. 1. Learn to listen well. The main difference between hearing and listening is that listening deals with understanding or perceiving sound waves, which have an in-depth meaning.

Salesforce Directory A AI Basics Common Industry Uses of AI KPI and AI by Industry Main Ingredients of AI Using AI in Business AI Ethics B-Z Salesforce Admin Salesforce Platform Basics Data Modeling Data Management Lightning Experience Mobile App User Engagement Reports and Dashboards for Lightning Experience Einstein AI Einstein AI for Sales Cloud Einstein Prediction Builder

Common Industry Uses Marketing AI has a huge impact on email marketing. There’s a few questions that you can ask in regards to email: - Will your customers open the email - Will they click through it - Will they act on it - Will they unsub All the questions above can be potentially predicted using Yes-and-No predictions. You can send emails to prospects who are likely to open them without ubsubing.

Salesforce offers two main methods for importing data. Data Import Wizard this tool, accessible through the Setup menu, lets you import data in common standard objects, such as contacts, leads, accounts, as well as data in custom objects. It can import up to 50,000 records at a time. It provides a simple interface to specify the configuration parameters, data sources, and the field mappings that map the field names in your import file with the field names in Salesforce.

A data model is a way to model what database tables look like in a way that makes sense to humans. In Salesforce, we think about database tables as objects, we think about columns as fields, and rows as records. Salesforce supports several different types of objects. There are: standard objects custom objects external objects platform events BigObjects Standard objects are objects that are included with Salesforce. Common business objects like Account, Contact, Lead, and Opportunity are all standard objects.

Process for Building Predictions Define your use case What problem is your company facing? Late payments; What type of prediction will help? Predict which invoices will be paid late; Can this prediction be phrased as a Yes/No or Numeric question? Will this invoice be paid late; How can this prediction be used? Assign account managers to follow up; How will success be measured? Percentage increase in on-time payments and percentage decrease in average number of days to pay.

Sakesforce Email Integrations and Sync You want to capture meetings, emails and contacts Storing conversations in salesforce is key to delivering SSOT and building innovation: Active Listening on All Channels and Activities; Capture Information; Managed Sales and Service Cycles; Automated Entries; Timely and Relevant Insights and Next Steps. Salesforce has many integration options today Retiring solutions: Salesforce for Outlook (phase out: June 2021-2023) allows manually log and/or sync events and contacts Installed software UI plugin and sync engine no auto email capture Lightning Sync (no New Orgs: Oct 2020, no definitive date for phase out)

Sales What they care about Opportunity to Win Ratio - Score Likelihood to Convert (Industry, Department, Location, Annual Revenue, Contact Title, Pitch Method) - Predict Time to Close (Industry, Department, Location, Annual Revenue, Contact Title, Number of Resources Assigned, Executive Sponsorship on Deal) - Surface Optimal Recommendations to Reps (Next best actions, such as if deal has been open for a long time, an executive should be on the call as well)

Main Ingredients of AI Yes-and-No Predictions Numeric Predictions Classifications Recommendations Yes-and-No Predictions generally comes in the form of a probability. These probabilities are sometimes converted into scores, such as on a numeric scale of 0 to 100. Numeric Predictions often power predictive forecasting, such as “how much revenue”, “how many days”. Classifications frequently use “deep learning” capabilities to operate on unstructured data like free text or images. Another type that may or may not use deep learning is “clustering”.

Salesforce Platform Basics Like any CRM, Salesforce stores your customer data, gives you processes to nurture prospective customers, and provides ways to collaborate with people you work with. Yet on top of that, it also comes with a lot of standard functionality, or out-of-the-box products and features that you can use to run your business. Sell to prospects and customers Leads and Opportunities to manage sales Help customers after the sale

Using AI in Business There are three steps to effectively utilizing AI in business. Decide What to Predict The first step in using AI is to figure out what you want to achieve. You can do so by clearly defining a measurable term that you are using to predict. Without a measurable term, it is impossible to make predictions. Get Historical Data in Order The second step is to obtain historical data.
